item.title: Holiday Open Studio - 03/26/2026 9:00am EDT to 04/02/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 1034 Township Rd 293, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA

I'm a formerly trained Northwest Ohio artist with a specialty in landscape paintings. On MONdays and Thursdays until Christmas, I’ll have my studio and gallery space at my rural home in the Middle Of Nowhere (MON) open to artists, art lovers, and enthusiasts to chat, and see what I have on my easel.

December sales. December treats. Expect art, wine, refreshments and goodies. Join me for some lively convo on all things art!

This "open studio day" started out as an accountability exercise on my part (to make sure I would be in my studio achieving my goals), but has since become a fun interaction I cherish.

To see more about my artwork : www.MONgallery.us

*Please drive all the way up between the studio door and the brown barn… easy parking for you. There are stairs down into the studio that may be hindering for those with mobility issues, but there is a handrail.
item.dc:creator: MONgallery and Art Studio

item.title: Pneumatics - 04/02/2026 8:00am UTC to 04/02/2026 4:00pm UTC
item.description: Location : 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA This course is a basic study of the principles of fluid power systems based on industrial applications. This course will have a heavy focus on pneumatic circuit operation and troubleshooting. The student will learn how to interpret a pneumatic circuit print and predict the operation of the circuit. Students will utilize the simulation software, Automation Studio, to understand how each pneumatic circuit operates. The student will also construct and troubleshoot the circuits in a controlled lab environment with industrial grade devices. The student will be introduced to hydraulic circuits, focusing on the safety of working with hydraulic systems and components, and interpreting hydraulic symbols in circuits. The student will also be introduced to controlling a pneumatic circuit with electrical/PLC circuits utilizing the simulation software in an asynchronous format.
item.dc:creator: Fostoria Learning Center

item.description: Location : 1925 W County Rd 26, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Hop on over to Fitch Homestead Farm Stand! Join us for a fun-filled Easter visit with the Easter Bunny from 10 AM – 12 PM! Bring the kiddos for a special meet & greet Grab a delicious cup of Shirley’s Coffee Stock up on festive Easter treats Make it an easy, one-stop stop for smiles, sweets, and springtime fun! Fitch Homestead Farm Stand We can’t wait to see you there!

item.title: Financial Fitness Friday - 04/03/2026 8:30am EDT to 04/03/2026 5:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 25 Shaffer Park Dr, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join our Financial Counselor Rusti Claydon for Financial Fitness Friday, a new monthly event for our membership for FREE walk-in assistance and education surrounding your financial concerns! The first Friday of each month, Rusti has cleared her schedule to allow for members of the credit union to stop in and ask questions about their situation, ask for insights into how we can assist and how to better utilize Your Legacy Federal Credit Union offerings to their benefit! Not a member? Rusti can get you started with just a $5 minimum initial share. See you there!
item.dc:creator: Your Legacy Federal Credit Union

item.description: Location : 7575 S Township Rd 131, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Come join us for an delicious fish dinner on April 3rd from 5:00 to 7:00 p.m. with our new way of releasing people to go up to eat we served 140 meals and under 45 minutes at our last fish fry so the lines aren't near as long and things just go a lot smoother. item.title: McCutchenville Recycling Drop Off collection weekend - 04/04/2026 12:00am EDT to 04/04/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description: Location : 8919 OH-53, McCutchenville, OH 44844, USA Join us for the monthly Recycling Drop Off Weekend, taking place on the 1st weekend of each month from Friday at 4 PM to Monday at 8 AM. You can drop off your recyclables at McCutchenville or Harpster, where we accept a variety of materials including aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, any color of glass, newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard, and books. Please ensure that shredded paper is bagged and that food and drink containers are rinsed clean, while caps can remain on the items. For more details, visit the respective links for McCutchenville and Harpster.
item.dc:creator: Wyandot County Recycling Center

item.title: Eclipse Ever After Anniversary Celebration - 04/04/2026 12:00pm EDT to 04/06/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The stars are aligning again… You’re Invited! Congratulations on celebrating one year of elopement and vow renewal bliss! We are thrilled to invite you back to Tiffin to relive the magic of Elope at the Eclipse and create even more unforgettable moments. Join us for a weekend filled with special events, unique experiences, and exciting activities designed just for you. Whether you are looking for a romantic getaway, a fun filled adventure, or a relaxing staycation right in your own backyard, Seneca County is the perfect destination to celebrate your love story, your way. Let the stars align once again for your love story! See the full lineup of events and offerings at: https://www.destinationsenecacounty.org/eclipse-ever.../ RSVP for Saturday Night's Eclipse Ever After Reunion: https://www.destinationsenecacounty.org/couples-reunion.../
item.dc:creator: Downtown Tiffin

item.title: Eclipse Ever After Anniversary Celebration - 04/04/2026 12:00pm EDT to 04/06/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The stars are aligning again… You’re Invited! Congratulations on celebrating one year of elopement and vow renewal bliss! We are thrilled to invite you back to Tiffin to relive the magic of Elope at the Eclipse and create even more unforgettable moments. Join us for a weekend filled with special events, unique experiences, and exciting activities designed just for you. Whether you are looking for a romantic getaway, a fun filled adventure, or a relaxing staycation right in your own backyard, Seneca County is the perfect destination to celebrate your love story, your way. Let the stars align once again for your love story! See the full lineup of events and offerings at: https://www.destinationsenecacounty.org/eclipse-ever.../ RSVP for Saturday Night's Eclipse Ever After Reunion: https://www.destinationsenecacounty.org/couples-reunion.../
item.dc:creator: Downtown Tiffin

item.title: Holiday Open Studio - 04/06/2026 9:00am EDT to 04/13/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 1034 Township Rd 293, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA

I'm a formerly trained Northwest Ohio artist with a specialty in landscape paintings. On MONdays and Thursdays until Christmas, I’ll have my studio and gallery space at my rural home in the Middle Of Nowhere (MON) open to artists, art lovers, and enthusiasts to chat, and see what I have on my easel.

December sales. December treats. Expect art, wine, refreshments and goodies. Join me for some lively convo on all things art!

This "open studio day" started out as an accountability exercise on my part (to make sure I would be in my studio achieving my goals), but has since become a fun interaction I cherish.

To see more about my artwork : www.MONgallery.us

*Please drive all the way up between the studio door and the brown barn… easy parking for you. There are stairs down into the studio that may be hindering for those with mobility issues, but there is a handrail.
item.dc:creator: MONgallery and Art Studio

item.title: Make it at Home: Felt Sunflower - 04/06/2026 9:30am EDT to 04/06/2026 10:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The Tiffin-Seneca Public Library’s virtual craft series for adults, “Make It At Home,” will offer free supply kits and an instructional video on how to make a felt sunflower beginning Monday, April 6. Patrons can pick up a supply kit at the Information Desk full of almost everything needed to make a craft each month, and will only need to use a few basic supplies from home. An instructional video on how to make the sunflower will be posted on the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library YouTube channel and Facebook page. Please note that kits will be available on a first-come, first-served basis, and registration is not required. One kit per adult. Must be present to receive your kit. This program is free for adults. If all kits are gone, the instruction video and information sheet can still be found at www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/aprilmakeitathome.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: "Still Life Arrangement & Painting" Mondays, April 6th and 13th, 6pm-9pm #04062602 - 04/06/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/13/2026 9:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Pre-registration and payment is required by March 30th. Paint your own still life with your favorite item and fruit or flowers. You will paint in water-mixable oils with Carol Hoffman on two Mondays April 6th and 13th from 6pm-9pm. These are 3 hour classes. Please arrive at least 15 minutes before class time. Carol will guide you through the process of creating this painting whether you are a beginner or more advanced painter. All of the materials including the canvas are included in the price of the class. Cost is $70 for TAG members & $80 for non-members. Call Carol with questions. 419-547-8610, Tiffin Art Guild at 419-443-0478 (tiffinartguild@att.net), or stop in at the Gallery, 130 S Washington St, Tiffin between Noon and 4 pm Wednesday thru Saturday.

item.title: How to Read a Book Like a Professor - 04/07/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/07/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA As part of the 2026 Christopher D. English Community Read, Tiffin-Seneca Public Library invites you to "How to Read a Book Like a Professor" on Tuesday, April 7 at 6:00 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room. Join Heidelberg professor Dr. Barry Devine for an engaging session where he will share techniques used by literature professors to analyze books, helping readers discover deeper themes and ask insightful questions. This event serves as a precursor to the Community Read book, The Astral Library by Kate Quinn, though prior reading of the book is not necessary. The Community Read initiative promotes reading and discussion in Seneca County through various special programs. Highlighting the event, bestselling author Kate Quinn will speak on Tuesday, April 28 at 6:30 p.m. at the Chandelier Community Event Center, followed by a book signing. Copies of The Astral Library will be available for purchase. Additional information about the Community Read can be found at www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/community-read.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Seneca County: A Land of Immigrants - 04/09/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/09/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Tiffin-Seneca Public Library invites you to "Seneca County: A Land of Immigrants" on Thursday, April 9 at 6:00 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room. Join Local History and Genealogy Specialist Jessica Hoehn for an engaging exploration of the rich cultural heritage of Seneca County. This program will delve into the stories of various immigrant groups who have settled in the area, highlighting their challenges and the lasting impact on community traditions, industries, and neighborhoods. The event is free and open to all adults and is part of T-SPL’s America 250 celebration. For more information, call (419) 447-3751 or visit www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Game Bird Workshop - 04/09/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/09/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 100 Hopewell Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Are you interested in raising pheasants, quail, or other game birds for sport, meat production, or conservation? This comprehensive workshop will provide you with the essential knowledge and practical skills needed to start and manage your own healthy flock. Whether you're a small-scale farmer, a future hunting preserve owner, or a hobbyist, join us to learn the best practices for successful game bird husbandry. This workshop is free but registration is required. Dinner will be provided starting at 5:30pm.

item.title: Poker Night at the Fire Station! - 04/11/2026 5:00pm EDT to 04/12/2026 12:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 120 Catherine St, Green Springs, OH 44836, USA Saturday Nov 8th Proceeds benefit the Independence day celebration! Doors open: 5pm cards play: 6pm No late entries! $30 buy in unlimited buy backs for first 3 rounds $10 buy-up for after 3rd round blinds double every 30 minutes bring a new player, you and them get an additional $100 in chips Starting stack $1k beer, pop, food will be available for purchase Pre-register and pay by 11/5 for an additional $100 in chips no late registrations after 6pm must be 18 call/ text 419-307-3675 to register Paypal and Square app Available for online payment through the fire department accounts. Only pay through posts from our page and NO OTHERS!

item.title: Holiday Open Studio - 04/13/2026 9:00am EDT to 04/20/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 1034 Township Rd 293, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA

I'm a formerly trained Northwest Ohio artist with a specialty in landscape paintings. On MONdays and Thursdays until Christmas, I’ll have my studio and gallery space at my rural home in the Middle Of Nowhere (MON) open to artists, art lovers, and enthusiasts to chat, and see what I have on my easel.

December sales. December treats. Expect art, wine, refreshments and goodies. Join me for some lively convo on all things art!

This "open studio day" started out as an accountability exercise on my part (to make sure I would be in my studio achieving my goals), but has since become a fun interaction I cherish.

To see more about my artwork : www.MONgallery.us

*Please drive all the way up between the studio door and the brown barn… easy parking for you. There are stairs down into the studio that may be hindering for those with mobility issues, but there is a handrail.
item.dc:creator: MONgallery and Art Studio

item.title: Allen Bradley PLC 1 - 04/14/2026 8:00am UTC to 04/14/2026 4:00pm UTC
item.description: Location : 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA The course is a study of the installation, programming and troubleshooting of programmable controlled systems currently used in an industrial environment. The focus will be on Installation, Programming, Engineering and Maintenance tasks performed with PLC systems. The primary PLC used for this class will be the Allen Bradley SLC-500 and CompactLogix, using RSLogix 500, RSLogix5000 and RSLinx software. The topics presented will be learned through Online instructional material, and hands on labs.

item.title: Seneca County Human Resources Association | April Meeting - 04/14/2026 11:45am EDT to 04/14/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 45 St Lawrence Dr, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Seneca County Human Resources Association for our April meeting on Tuesday, April 14, 2026, from 11:45 a.m. to 1:00 p.m. in the McMullen Conference Room at Mercy Health – Tiffin Hospital. We will be joined by Renee Smith and Sarah Stephens Krupp of Thrive Community Partners. In this session, Thrive Community Partners will share real examples from local employers who identified specific, on-demand needs and how targeted training helped address them. Participants will walk through a simple “on-demand training loop” they can adapt inside their own organizations to quickly respond to emerging workforce challenges. The Seneca County Human Resources Association (SCHRA) connects HR professionals, business leaders, and organizational managers across Seneca County to share best practices, discuss workforce trends, and grow professionally. Meetings focus on practical learning, peer discussion, and updates on programs and resources that support hiring, engagement, compliance, and organizational success. Membership in the Seneca County Human Resources Association (SCHRA) is open to anyone with HR responsibilities or an interest in workforce and personnel development. SCHRA operates on a one-time yearly registration, allowing members to sign up once for the entire year rather than registering for each individual meeting. Registered members receive meeting notices, speaker details, and calendar invitations for every upcoming session throughout the year. To register for SCHRA visit: https://www.developsenecacounty.org/schra-interest-form/
item.dc:creator: Seneca Regional Chamber & Development

item.title: 3p | Teen Tuesday - 04/14/2026 3:00pm EDT to 04/14/2026 4:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA null

item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA “Teen Tuesdays” will be returning to the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library every Tuesday from 3:00 p.m. to 4:30 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room beginning Tuesday, September 2. Teens in grades 6 through 12 are invited to a weekly afterschool hangout for games, snacks, crafts and more. For more information, log onto www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org or call the library at 419-447-3751.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: T-SPL Talks: Treatment Foster Care - 04/14/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/14/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join Tiffin-Seneca Public Library on Tuesday, April 14 at 6:00 p.m. for "T-SPL Talks: Treatment Foster Care," part of the 2026 Christopher D. English Community Read. This event, held in the Frost Kalnow Room, features a presentation from Northwest Ohio Treatment Foster Care, one of this year's Community Impact Partners. Attendees will learn about treatment foster care, its role in supporting children with higher needs, the requirements to become a foster caregiver, and how the community can get involved. The Community Read centers around the book "The Astral Library" by Kate Quinn, promoting unity through reading and discussions. In addition, best-selling author Kate Quinn will speak at a free community event on Tuesday, April 28 at 6:30 p.m. at the Chandelier Community Event Center, followed by a book signing. For more information about the Community Read and upcoming programs, visit www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/community-read.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Freedom Rising XII - 04/16/2026 5:00pm EDT to 04/16/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 151 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA ANNOUNCEMENT: FREEDOM RISING XII We are thrilled to officially announce Freedom Rising XII, our annual celebration and largest fundraiser of the year benefiting Sisters in Shelter and it’s an evening you won’t want to miss. This powerful night brings our community together in support of survivors of human trafficking through an unforgettable program that includes: Inspiring program & mission updates Raffle & Silent Auction (with over 100 incredible items last year!) Heavy hors d’oeuvres Cash bar Keynote Speaker: Brittany Joy Figlar Brittany’s story is one of courage, resilience, and transformation. Each time she speaks, she moves hearts and challenges us to see what’s possible when survivors are empowered to rise from victim to victor. Get Involved: • Interested in donating a raffle or silent auction item or basket? We would be deeply grateful for your support. • Sponsorship opportunities are available at multiple levels, offering meaningful ways to stand with survivors while showing your commitment to our mission. $1,000 Gold Sponsorship includes: 1 VIP Table (8 seats) 8 Tickets to event 8 Free drink tickets 2 Sisters in Shelter T-Shirts Swag bag Advertising on Social Media, Newspaper, Printed Materials, and Radio! $500 Silver Sponsorship includes: 4 Tickets to event 4 Free drink tickets Swag bag Advertising on Social Media, Newspaper, Printed Materials, and Radio! • And even if sponsorship isn’t the right fit, every donation, at any amount, makes a difference. We truly could not do this without our community. We are so blessed to live in a place that shows up for local businesses, for organizations, and most importantly… for people. Tickets are now available (https://www.zeffy.com/en-US/ticketing/freedom-rising-xii ) join us for a night of purpose, impact, and hope. This is more than an event. It’s a movement. We look forward to connecting with you: Sisters in Shelter Any Questions or Contact information: 201 S. Washington Street, PO Box 384 Tiffin, Ohio 44883 (567) 205-8771 sistersinshelter@gmail.com
item.dc:creator: Sisters in Shelter

item.title: Freedom Rising XII Celebration and Fundraiser - 04/16/2026 5:00pm EDT to 04/16/2026 7:30pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 151 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Join community and business leaders for an unforgettable evening with Sisters in Shelter at their Freedom Rising XII, 5:00-7:30 pm Thursday April 16, 2026, at The Chandelier Community Event Center, 151 S. Washington Street, Tiffin.

 

The night will include a keynote presentation from Brittany Joy Figlar who brings a voice to the stage that is both courageous and unforgettable. A survivor of human trafficking, Brittany has transformed her lived experience into powerful advocacy—educating communities, training professionals, and challenging systems to better protect and support survivors. Her story is raw, honest, and deeply moving, reminding us not only of the realities of exploitation, but of the strength and resilience it takes to rise beyond it. Hearing Brittany speak is not just inspiring, it’s a moment that stays with you long after the night ends.

 

Your support at this event will directly contribute to critical support services provided for survivors which include shelter, counseling, legal aid, and vocational training; and to expand awareness campaigns by educating community members to recognize the signs of human trafficking and how to report it.

Tickets for the Freedom Rising XII Celebration and Fundraiser are available now.  There will be a cash bar & heavy hors d'oeuvres.  There will be 50/50, raffles, and silent auction with all proceeds to benefit Sisters in Shelter. Business attire is appropriate. Don't miss this opportunity to be a part of something truly impactful. Together we can make a difference.

 

Sisters in Shelter, which began in 2005, is a non-profit organization that supports survivors and illuminate the atrocities of human trafficking through community education and advocacy. Sisters in Shelter provides critical first needs for women survivors of human trafficking: emergency safe housing, medical care, safety planning, and legal advocacy. To donate directly to Sisters in Shelter, or to learn about volunteer opportunities, visit sistersinshelter.com or visit Facebook: Sisters in Shelter.
item.dc:creator: Seneca County Commission on Aging and Sisters in Shelter

item.title: "The Abstract Show" will be displayed from April 1 -25. Intake will be from March 25-28. - 04/16/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/30/2026 8:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Get ready for a bold, vibrant, and unpredictable art experience! The Abstract Art Exhibit is all about letting go, embracing creativity, and seeing the world through an entirely new lens. Whether you're an artist or an admirer, this is a show that will challenge, inspire, and excite both the creator and the viewer! Abstract art is more than just shapes and colors, it's about emotion, movement, and limitless expression. Every piece in this exhibit pushes the boundaries of imagination, drawing you into a world where rules are meant to be broken, and where creativity knows no limits. Expect a dazzling mix of mediums, including photography, painting, sculpture, textiles, digital art, jewelry, pastels, glass, and more. If you can dream it, it belongs here! Whether youÕre showcasing your work or simply soaking in the creativity, donÕt miss this one-of-a kind exhibit. Step inside, explore, and let your imagination run wild!
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Art Guild

item.title: Into the Woods - 04/16/2026 7:00pm EDT to 04/16/2026 9:30pm EDT
item.description:
Location : University Hall, 310 E Market St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Heidelberg University School of Music and Theatre presents Into the Woods.

Be careful what you wish for... 🌲✨

Heidelberg University School of Music and Theatre invites you to a spellbinding production of Stephen Sondheim and James Lapine’s Into the Woods. Watch as the worlds of Cinderella, Little Red Riding Hood, and Jack and the Beanstalk collide in a darkly comic exploration of what happens after "happily ever after."

Join a baker and his wife on their quest to break a witch's curse—and discover the true cost of getting everything you ever wanted.

📍 Where: Gundlach Theatre 

📅 When: * April 16, 17, & 18 | 7:00 PM

April 19 | 2:00 PM

Tickets:

 https://www.eventbrite.com/cc/into-the-woods-4810285

Poster Design by Bri Clark, BA’23 and MBA’24

Into the Woods is presented through special arrangement with Music Theatre International (MTI).

 All authorized performance materials are also supplied by MTI.

www.mtishows.com
item.dc:creator: Heidelberg University School of Music and Theatre

item.title: Cold Case Analysis and Community Impact: Inside TU’s Fellowship Program - 04/17/2026 12:00am EDT to 04/17/2026 1:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 155 Miami St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The upcoming event features a two-part discussion focusing on significant historical and social themes. The first segment, led by Dr. Michael Curtis, the Director of Tiffin University's Cold Case Fellowship, will analyze the impact of cold case investigations on communities, highlighting the experiences of students involved in the fellowship program. The second segment will be conducted by Dr. Susan Trollinger, a Professor of English and Rhetoric at the University of Dayton, who will explore the embodied rhetoric employed by women during the suffrage movement, examining the ways in which their attire and presentation influenced public perception and mobilization for women's rights. Together, these presentations will offer insights into the intersection of academic inquiry, community engagement, and historical advocacy.
item.dc:creator: LIFE@TU

item.title: 13th Annual Protective Services 5K - 04/18/2026 8:30am EDT to 04/18/2026 11:30am EDT
item.description:
Location : 491 Coe St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Join Us for the 13th Annual Protective Services 5K!

Get ready to lace up your sneakers and hit the pavement with fellow enthusiasts at this fun, in-person event to promote child abuse awareness and prevention. Whether you're a seasoned runner or prefer to walk, the 13th Annual Protective Services 5K is perfect for everyone. Bring your dog too! Come enjoy the energy, the community spirit, and show your support! Don’t miss out on the action—see you at the starting line! We will start at the American Legion Shelter at Hedges Boyer Park and follow the Rock Creek Trail.

We've experienced some sprinkles in years past and we don't let that stop us. In the event of significant rain, the event will be held at Frann's Fieldhouse, on the Heidelberg campus. Please watch our Facebook page (Seneca County JFS) for updates.

Dogs are welcome. Please have them on a leash and be sure to pick up anything they leave behind.

This is a free event.

8:30 am: Registration and packet pick-up

8:50 am: Opening Remarks

9: 00 am: Runners Start

9:05 am: Walkers Start
item.dc:creator: Seneca County JFS

item.title: Rick Springfield - 04/18/2026 7:30pm EDT to 04/18/2026 9:30pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 30 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Over the past four decades, Rick Springfield has worn many hats as an entertainer and performer. The creator of some of the finest power-pop of the ’80s, a Grammy winning singer, songwriter, and musician who has sold 25 million albums and scored 17 U.S. Top 40 hits, including “Jessie’s Girl,” “Don’t Talk to Strangers,” “An Affair of the Heart,” “I’ve Done Everything for You,” “Love Somebody,” and “Human Touch.” He’s an accomplished actor who has starred opposite Meryl Streep in the feature film “Ricki and the Flash,” gave a chameleonic performance as the creepy Dr. Irving Pitlor in HBO’s prestige drama “True Detective,” earned great reviews for his portrayal of Lucifer on the CW hit “Supernatural” and most recently played the insane Pastor Charles on FX’s worldwide hit series “American Horror Story.” An author as well, both his candid 2010 memoir Late, Late at Night (which Rolling Stone named one of the 25 greatest rock memoirs of all time) and his 2014 comedic novel Magnificent Vibration earned rave reviews and spots on the New York Times Best Sellers’ list. In 2014, Springfield was honored with a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, located around the corner from the first apartment he lived in when he first arrived in the U.S. from Australia in 1971. Springfield’s Sirius-XM radio show, Working Class DJ, is a huge hit and airs weekly on 80s on 8. His new album “Automatic” is out now. Automatic is his first album of all new original material in 5 years.

$125, $100, $75

Fan Club tickets go on sale November 10 at 10:00 AM

Public ticket sales begin November 17 at 10:00 AM.
item.dc:creator: The Ritz Theatre

item.title: Holiday Open Studio - 04/20/2026 9:00am EDT to 04/27/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 1034 Township Rd 293, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA

I'm a formerly trained Northwest Ohio artist with a specialty in landscape paintings. On MONdays and Thursdays until Christmas, I’ll have my studio and gallery space at my rural home in the Middle Of Nowhere (MON) open to artists, art lovers, and enthusiasts to chat, and see what I have on my easel.

December sales. December treats. Expect art, wine, refreshments and goodies. Join me for some lively convo on all things art!

This "open studio day" started out as an accountability exercise on my part (to make sure I would be in my studio achieving my goals), but has since become a fun interaction I cherish.

To see more about my artwork : www.MONgallery.us

*Please drive all the way up between the studio door and the brown barn… easy parking for you. There are stairs down into the studio that may be hindering for those with mobility issues, but there is a handrail.
item.dc:creator: MONgallery and Art Studio

item.title: Ribbon Cutting | Buckeye Community Schools - 04/20/2026 4:00pm EDT to 04/20/2026 5:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 870 W Market St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Seneca Regional Chamber & Development Ambassadors in celebrating the grand opening of Buckeye Community Schools’ Tiffin location with a ribbon cutting and open house on April 20, 2026, from 4–6 PM. Buckeye Community Schools is a public charter high school serving students ages 14–21, offering an alternative educational environment designed to help students succeed on their own terms. With a unique paper-and-pencil curriculum and a personalized approach, each student is paired with the same instructor throughout their educational journey, building strong relationships, consistency, and support. BCS prides itself on creating a different kind of learning experience, meeting students where they are and empowering them to achieve their academic goals in a supportive and flexible setting. Guests are encouraged to stay for the open house, tour the new facility, and learn more about the impact Buckeye Community Schools is making in Seneca County.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Regional Chamber & Development

item.title: CASA Volunteer Training Orientation - 04/21/2026 5:00pm EDT to 04/21/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 21 Court St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Imagine being a child, moving from home to home… Never knowing who to trust. Never having someone to speak up for you. Now imagine YOU being the person who changes that story. A steady voice. A safe place. A lifeline in a difficult time. Our Spring Volunteer Training Orientation is Tuesday, April 21 at 5:00 PM at our Tiffin office (21 Court Street). For about 8 hours a month, you can help a child look back on their childhood with gratitude instead of regret. No legal background required — just heart, time, and a commitment to stand by a child when they need it most. Learn more or apply at www.casaofssw.org or call 419-448-1442. Because right now, there are children who desperately need you.
item.dc:creator: CASA of SSW

item.title: Holiday Open Studio - 04/23/2026 9:00am EDT to 04/30/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 1034 Township Rd 293, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA

I'm a formerly trained Northwest Ohio artist with a specialty in landscape paintings. On MONdays and Thursdays until Christmas, I’ll have my studio and gallery space at my rural home in the Middle Of Nowhere (MON) open to artists, art lovers, and enthusiasts to chat, and see what I have on my easel.

December sales. December treats. Expect art, wine, refreshments and goodies. Join me for some lively convo on all things art!

This "open studio day" started out as an accountability exercise on my part (to make sure I would be in my studio achieving my goals), but has since become a fun interaction I cherish.

To see more about my artwork : www.MONgallery.us

*Please drive all the way up between the studio door and the brown barn… easy parking for you. There are stairs down into the studio that may be hindering for those with mobility issues, but there is a handrail.
item.dc:creator: MONgallery and Art Studio

item.title: LIFEGUARDING COURSE - 04/24/2026 12:00am EDT to 04/26/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 180 Summit St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Ready to make a difference this summer? 🌊☀️

Become a certified lifeguard and gain the skills to save lives, build confidence, and become a leader!

Important Details:

APRIL 24-26, 2026

Friday 4p-8p, Saturday 8a-5p, & Sunday 8a-5p

• Blended course format – 7 hours of online coursework must be completed before the first day of class

• Swim assessment required one week prior to the first class- Friday at 6pm

• Attendance is mandatory for all scheduled days (no make-up sessions)

• Registration closes one week before the class begins

• Limited to just 8 students per class – reserve your spot early!

Don’t wait—spots fill fast! 📅 Enroll now at the Tiffin Community YMCA and dive into a new opportunity!

item.title: Wharton Recycling Drop Off Collection Weekend - 04/25/2026 12:00am EDT to 04/25/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description: Location : 11385 Co Hwy 4, Carey, OH 43316, USA Join us for the Recycling Drop Off Collection Weekend, held monthly on the 4th weekend from Friday at 4 PM to Monday at 8 AM. This event takes place at various locations, including Wharton and Kirby, where you can drop off your recyclables. Accepted items include aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, any color of glass, newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard, and books. Please ensure that shredded paper is bagged and that food and drink containers are rinsed clean; caps can remain on the items. For more details, visit our event calendar.
item.dc:creator: Wyandot County Recycling Center

item.title: Downtown Tiffin Clean Up ~ April 2026 - 04/25/2026 9:00am EDT to 04/25/2026 10:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for a Downtown Tiffin Clean-Up on Saturday, April 25, 2026, from 9:00–10:30 AM! We’ll meet at Cherry Alley, and we’ll have everything you need (trash bags, brooms, dust-pans, and gloves). Who can volunteer? Everyone! Come on your own, bring a friend, or build a group and join "Bunky’s Litter League", a community-wide initiative focused on litter cleanup and beautification. Teams can include: Businesses Neighborhood groups Faith-based organizations Civic and service groups Clubs Families, individuals, and more... Volunteers are what make this event possible. Your help keeps downtown clean, welcoming, and vibrant. Please register to volunteer by clicking the “Ticket” tab. Save the dates (more clean-ups!) June 27, 2026 September 12, 2026 November 21, 2026 (Leaf Clean-Up)
item.dc:creator: Downtown Tiffin

item.title: Day Of Giving For The Famous Aunt Ellen! - 04/26/2026 8:00am EDT to 04/26/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : Tiffin, OH 44883, USA If you know of Big Mike's, then you most definitely know The Famous Aunt Ellen. Words one could use to describe her are strong, faithful, funny, caring, kind, and selfless. Always been one to help someone else where I think accepting help is a hard thing for her to take. I am grateful that she has said yes to allowing us to put this event on for her to show her just how much we love her and want to help her in this battle! For anyone who does not know, Aunt Ellen was diagnosed with pancreatic cancer in the fall. She is currently doing chemotherapy and is responding really well to it thus far. We are hoping to raise money to help Aunt Ellen with medical bills during this difficult time. If you wish to donate, please reach out to me so I can add you on our list. We will be selling $10 raffle tickets for items to win with all the proceeds going right to The Famous Aunt Ellen! I know items already donated are a 2 hour ride party bike ride and 1 night stay at The Melmore Cabin! Many more cool prizes will be announced as they come in! We will be serving food all day long. We hope to have entertainment for kids and a band on the roof again! We will announce these options as we get them confirmed!
item.dc:creator: Big Mike's

item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Tiffin Seneca Public Library for the adult book discussion group, “Let’s Talk About It,” on Monday, April 27 at 10:30 a.m. in the Junior Home Room. The group will explore the 2026 Community Read selection, The Astral Library by Kate Quinn. Copies of the book are available at the Library, and everyone is welcome to attend this free event without registration. Refreshments will be provided. For more details, please call the Library at 419-447-3751 or visit their online calendar at www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org.

item.title: Homeschool Genre Book Club - 04/27/2026 1:00pm EDT to 04/27/2026 2:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library for the "Homeschool Genre Book Club," designed for homeschool students aged 8 to 18, on Monday, April 27, from 1:00 to 2:00 p.m. Students aged 8-12 will gather in the Kernan Room, while those aged 13-18 will meet in the Junior Home Room. Each month features a different genre, with this month's focus on poetry and novels in verse. Participants are encouraged to read a selected book before the meeting and share their thoughts during the discussion. Following the conversation, engaging genre-related activities will be held. For book recommendations, including options available on hoopla, visit the Youth Services Desk. For more information, call (419) 447-3751 or visit www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org.

item.title: 24th Annual Good Samaritan Dinner & Auction - 04/29/2026 4:30pm EDT to 04/29/2026 5:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 631 Plaza Dr, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Dear Friends, We are pleased to announce the 24th Annual Good Shepherd Home Good Samaritan Dinner & Auction to be held on Wednesday, April 29, 2026. This year’s theme, “A Community in Bloom,” celebrates the compassion, generosity, and shared spirit that continue to strengthen our mission. The evening will once again be held at Stacy’s Place, 625 Plaza Drive, Fostoria. We warmly invite you to attend the dinner and/or contribute to the success of this special event, which helps raise funds for residents whose financial resources have been exhausted. As in previous years, all donors and sponsors will be recognized in our event program. Tickets are available for $40 per person and include appetizers, a seated dinner, dessert, and drinks. VIP tables are available for $400 and include seating for eight ( guests. In addition to donating auction items, we are excited to introduce our Blooming Sponsor Levels for those who wish to support the event through a monetary contribution: Violet Sponsor – $25 Rose Sponsor - $300 Daisy Sponsor – $75 Grand Garden Sponsor - $500 Tulip Sponsor – $150 Monetary sponsorships are gratefully accepted for those who wish to support the event in lieu of an item to contribute. To reserve tickets or inquire about sponsorship opportunities, please contact Crystal Macias at 419-937-1801 or cmacias@goodshepherdhome.com. If you would like to donate an auction item, please contact Wahneta Mockensturm at 419-937-1801 or wmockensturm@goodshepherdhome.com. Items may be dropped off, or we are happy to arrange pickup. On behalf of the Good Shepherd Home staff and the residents we serve, thank you for your continued generosity and support.
item.dc:creator: Good Shepherd Home

item.description: Location : 25 Shaffer Park Dr, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join our Financial Counselor Rusti Claydon for Financial Fitness Friday, a new monthly event for our membership for FREE walk-in assistance and education surrounding your financial concerns! The first Friday of each month, Rusti has cleared her schedule to allow for members of the credit union to stop in and ask questions about their situation, ask for insights into how we can assist and how to better utilize Your Legacy Federal Credit Union offerings to their benefit! Not a member? Rusti can get you started with just a $5 minimum initial share. See you there!

item.description: Location : 8919 OH-53, McCutchenville, OH 44844, USA Join us for the monthly Recycling Drop-Off collection weekends in McCutchenville and Harpster, running from Friday at 4 PM to Monday at 8 AM on the first weekend of every month. You can recycle aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, any color of glass, newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard, and books. Please ensure that shredded paper is bagged and that food and drink containers are rinsed clean, although you may leave the caps on your items. For more details, visit the Wyandot County calendar at the links provided: [McCutchenville](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2366) and [Harpster](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2314).

item.title: Encounter the Heart of Jesus: Adoration & Healing Prayer - 05/03/2026 6:30pm EDT to 05/03/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 323 N Wood St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA "'I will restore your health, I will heal your wounds,' says the Lord" - Jeremiah 30:17 God still desires to heal today! Encounter the Heart of Jesus evenings of Eucharistic Adoration will include communal prayer for healing both physical & spiritual wounds, as well as the opportunity for individual prayer ministry & the Sacrament of Confession, within the context of Eucharistic Adoration. St. Augustine said, "the Church is the traveler's inn where the wounded are healed." Please pray about who in your life (a friend, family member, coworker, neighbor) might be in need of the Lord's healing and invite them to come and encounter the Lord and experience His work in their lives. The Lord truly does desire to heal anything in our lives that keeps us from full freedom & life in Him! Come & see what the Lord wants to do in your life! ***Please note that December 7th & March 1st will be our parish's Advent/ Lent Penance Services in the context of Eucharistic Adoration and will NOT include prayer for healing.
item.dc:creator: St. Wendelin Parish

item.title: Holiday Open Studio - 05/04/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/11/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 1034 Township Rd 293, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA

I'm a formerly trained Northwest Ohio artist with a specialty in landscape paintings. On MONdays and Thursdays until Christmas, I’ll have my studio and gallery space at my rural home in the Middle Of Nowhere (MON) open to artists, art lovers, and enthusiasts to chat, and see what I have on my easel.

December sales. December treats. Expect art, wine, refreshments and goodies. Join me for some lively convo on all things art!

This "open studio day" started out as an accountability exercise on my part (to make sure I would be in my studio achieving my goals), but has since become a fun interaction I cherish.

To see more about my artwork : www.MONgallery.us

*Please drive all the way up between the studio door and the brown barn… easy parking for you. There are stairs down into the studio that may be hindering for those with mobility issues, but there is a handrail.
item.dc:creator: MONgallery and Art Studio

item.title: Nonprofit Leadership Alliance | Healthy Teams, Strong Missions - 05/08/2026 11:00am EDT to 05/08/2026 12:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 928 W Market St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Seneca Regional Chamber & Development and United Way of North Central Ohio for the second Nonprofit Leadership Alliance quarterly meeting of 2026 on Friday, May 8 at 11 AM, hosted at North Central Ohio ESC. This session is designed for nonprofit leaders, staff, and board members who want to better support themselves and their teams while sustaining meaningful, mission-driven work. The featured topic, “Healthy Teams, Strong Missions: Preventing Burnout & Supporting Mental Well-Being,” will focus on creating workplace cultures that recognize stress, prioritize well-being, and prevent burnout. Presenters Erin Perry, MA, LPCC-S, owner of Counseling Connections Center, and Kim Radison, owner of KKR Consult, will share practical strategies nonprofit organizations can use to promote mental health, build resilience, and support staff at all levels. Attendees will learn how to recognize common signs of stress and burnout, apply self-care and resilience practices, and foster healthier, more supportive work environments. This session is designed to offer realistic, actionable tools that can be implemented immediately within nonprofit teams. Following the presentation, Erin Perry and Kim Radison will offer attendees the opportunity to schedule a free one-on-one session for additional personalized support or consultation. These meetings are free and open to all nonprofit employees and board members in Seneca County. Chamber & Development membership is not required. Join us for an important conversation about caring for the people behind the mission. Register today to secure your spot.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Regional Chamber & Development

item.title: "Trumpeter Swan" Saturday, May 9, 2026 9am-1pm #05092601 - 05/09/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/09/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Pre-registration and payment is required by May 2nd. Paint "Trumpeter Swan" in acrylic with Carol Hoffman on Saturday, May 9 from 9 am until 1 pm. This is a 4 hour class. Please arrive at least 15 minutes before class time. Carol will guide you through the process of creating this painting whether you are a beginner or more advanced painter. All of the materials including the 12x16" canvas are included in the price of the class. Please bring a large box to take your painting home in. Cost is $55 for TAG members & $65 for non-members. Call Carol with questions. 419-547-8610, Tiffin Art Guild at 419-443-0478 (tiffinartguild@att.net), or stop in at the Gallery, 130 S Washington St, Tiffin between Noon and 4 pm Wednesday thru Saturday.

item.description: Location : Aeraland Recreational Area, 1220 Township Rd 243, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Many interesting and colorful birds are passing through during spring migration on their way to the breeding grounds in the north. During this guided hike, search for birds like Eastern Meadowlark, Orchard Oriole, Indigo Bunting, Red-eyed Vireo, and a wide range of colorful warblers. Binoculars will be provided or bring your own. Aeraland Recreation Area Shelter 9 AM All ages are welcome. Free; registration is not required. Tim Kleman tkleman@hancockparks.com

item.title: "Colored Pencil Mountain Trees" Monday, May 11, 2026 12pm-3pm #05112601 - 05/11/2026 12:00pm EDT to 05/11/2026 3:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA In this class you will use colored pencils to create a 5"x7" landscape drawing of mountain trees, learning about strokes and blending techniques. All supplies are provided. Preregistration is required by Friday, May 8. Minimum class size is 3 people. The cost is $45 for TAG members & $55 for non-members. Contact Mike Fink (813-695-0506), or TAG (419-443-0478), or tiffinartguild@att.net with questions. You can also stop in at the Gallery, 130 S Washington St, Tiffin between Noon and 4 pm Wednesday thru Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Art Guild

item.title: Buckeye Lady Beetle Blitz Community Science Program Training - 05/11/2026 6:00pm EDT to 05/11/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Buckeye Lady Beetle Blitz Community Science Program Training 2026 Lady beetles are important insect predators that feed on landscape and agricultural pests. Unfortunately, some of our native Ohio lady beetles are in decline. YOU can help us track remaining populations! - You must have a home garden- food or flower - You will collect for 1 week in June and 1 week in August - We provide all the materials Interested? Register at go.osu.edu/blbbseneca
item.dc:creator: Ohio State University Extension - Seneca County

item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for the next session of the Downtown Tiffin Town Hall Series: Connecting People, Ideas, and Progress on Wednesday, May 13, 2026, hosted by the Design Committee at 9 AM. This session, titled “Design Matters: Creating a Downtown That Works and Welcomes,” will focus on design, beautification, and infrastructure, including parking. We’ll explore opportunities to improve downtown’s appearance and functionality through better signage, accessibility, and creative solutions for parking and walkability, all aimed at making Downtown Tiffin more inviting, accessible, and vibrant.

item.title: Holiday Open Studio - 05/14/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/21/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 1034 Township Rd 293, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA

I'm a formerly trained Northwest Ohio artist with a specialty in landscape paintings. On MONdays and Thursdays until Christmas, I’ll have my studio and gallery space at my rural home in the Middle Of Nowhere (MON) open to artists, art lovers, and enthusiasts to chat, and see what I have on my easel.

December sales. December treats. Expect art, wine, refreshments and goodies. Join me for some lively convo on all things art!

This "open studio day" started out as an accountability exercise on my part (to make sure I would be in my studio achieving my goals), but has since become a fun interaction I cherish.

To see more about my artwork : www.MONgallery.us

*Please drive all the way up between the studio door and the brown barn… easy parking for you. There are stairs down into the studio that may be hindering for those with mobility issues, but there is a handrail.
item.dc:creator: MONgallery and Art Studio

item.description: Location : 151 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Groups of all ages are invited to compete in two rounds of general trivia questions plus the final questions of local interest. Answers will be judged by local celebrities. Participants are asked to form teams of 6 or fewer, which can be composed of all ages. Teams will write their answers to be judged by local celebrities. Questions will be geared toward ages 12 and up. No phones, tablets, or electronic devices may be accessed during the contest. A traveling trophy will be awarded to the winning team. **ONE GrowthZone ticket per team. List each of the team members on the ticket.

item.title: LIFEGUARDING COURSE - 05/15/2026 12:00am EDT to 05/17/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 180 Summit St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Ready to make a difference this summer? 🌊☀️

Become a certified lifeguard and gain the skills to save lives, build confidence, and become a leader!

Important Details:

MAY 15-17, 2026

Friday 4p-8p, Saturday 8a-5p, & Sunday 8a-5p

• Blended course format – 7 hours of online coursework must be completed before the first day of class

• Swim assessment required one week prior to the first class- Friday at 6pm

• Attendance is mandatory for all scheduled days (no make-up sessions)

• Registration closes one week before the class begins

• Limited to just 8 students per class – reserve your spot early!

Don’t wait—spots fill fast! 📅 Enroll now at the Tiffin Community YMCA and dive into a new opportunity!

item.title: 2026 Art in the Park: Plein Air Gatherings - 05/21/2026 2:00pm EDT to 05/21/2026 4:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : Aeraland Recreational Area, 1220 Township Rd 243, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Plein Air Gatherings are an opportunity for artists to get together and paint or draw outdoors “en plein air” in Hancock County parks. All adult artists who use any medium conducive to working outdoors are welcome. If you are new to plein air painting or are hesitant to go out on your own, these gatherings are a perfect opportunity to meet and work with other plein air artists. Please join us for our 13th season! You should plan to bring whatever supplies and equipment you need and dress for ground and weather conditions. There is no fee or registration. It is not necessary to attend every session – come when you can. In case of rain, the session is cancelled. Gatherings will be held weekly on Thursdays. For more information or questions, please contact volunteer coordinator Sharon Hammer Baker at bakerartstudio@gmail.com or HPD Program Specialist Tim Kleman at tkleman@hancockparks.com.
item.dc:creator: Hancock Park District

item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for a paw-some evening in Downtown Tiffin on Thursday, May 21, 2026, from 5 to 8 PM! Enjoy a pet-friendly night out with your furry friends as you stroll through downtown and discover a pet-friendly experience throughout the downtown district. Stay tuned for more details and a list of participating businesses! If your dog is aggressive toward other dogs, children, or strangers, we kindly ask that you leave them at home. All dogs must be leashed, and owners are responsible for bringing doggie bags and cleaning up after their pets. Mark your calendars and plan to join us for a fun and pet-friendly evening in Downtown Tiffin!

item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA We've opened the doors to provide a better member experience for our chamber & development members and to help you get the most of your membership, ask questions, and get help to elevate your business! Chamber & Development Chat and Assist is an opportunity for our members to meet one on one with a Chamber & Development team member to understand and take advantage everything that comes with their membership, the opportunity to get help with anything from social media tips to Canva crash courses and beyond, to providing feedback to us so we can get better! Upcoming 2026 Chat & Assist dates include: Wednesday, June 24, 2026 from 10 AM to 1 PM Wednesday, July 22, 2026 from 10 AM to 1 PM Wednesday, August 26, 2026 from 10 AM to 1 PM Wednesday, September 23, 2026 from 10 AM to 1 PM Wednesday, October 28, 2026 from 10 AM to 1 PM You can secure a time (this is just so we know who to expect and how to best prepare!) at https://calendar.google.com/.../AcZssZ2Jz5jSs5OdbQ7h4VKho....

item.title: LIFEGUARDING COURSE - 05/29/2026 12:00am EDT to 05/31/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 180 Summit St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Ready to make a difference this summer? 🌊☀️

Become a certified lifeguard and gain the skills to save lives, build confidence, and become a leader!

Important Details:

MAY 29-31, 2026

Friday 4p-8p, Saturday 8a-5p, & Sunday 8a-5p

• Blended course format – 7 hours of online coursework must be completed before the first day of class

• Swim assessment required one week prior to the first class- Friday at 6pm

• Attendance is mandatory for all scheduled days (no make-up sessions)

• Registration closes one week before the class begins

• Limited to just 8 students per class – reserve your spot early!

Don’t wait—spots fill fast! 📅 Enroll now at the Tiffin Community YMCA and dive into a new opportunity!
item.dc:creator: YMCA of Bucyrus-Tiffin, Inc

item.title: Ohio Rural Business Initiative - 05/29/2026 10:00am EDT to 05/29/2026 12:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the U.S. Small Business Administration for the Ohio Rural Business Initiative, an in person event designed to support rural based small businesses across all industries. This session will focus on helping businesses understand available resources, access capital, and connect directly with agencies and partners working to support rural economic growth. Is your rural business ready for the next level? The Ohio Rural Business Initiative brings together the SBA and partner organizations to share tools, funding opportunities, and guidance tailored specifically for rural businesses. Attendees will learn what programs are available, how to navigate funding options step by step, and how different agencies are working together to support business growth in rural communities. This is also a great opportunity to connect with officials, resource providers, and fellow rural business owners who are navigating similar challenges and opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Regional Chamber & Development

item.description: Location : 670 OH-18, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Autumnwood Care Center’s 2nd Annual Motorcycle & Car Show Join us for a great afternoon of vehicles, food, and community while supporting a meaningful cause! Saturday, May 30 11:00 AM – 1:00 PM Autumnwood Care Center 607 E State Route 18 Tiffin, Ohio 44883 This event will benefit the Seneca County Honor Bus, helping local veterans travel to Washington, D.C. to visit the memorials built in their honor. Event Highlights: • Motorcycle & Car Show • Cookout • 50/50 Raffle • Gift Basket Raffle All activities are by donation and the event is open to the public. Bring your friends, family, and your favorite ride and help us support our veterans! Questions? Call 419-447-7151
item.dc:creator: Autumnwood Care Center

item.title: 2026 Volunteer Orientation Seminar - 05/30/2026 1:00pm EDT to 05/30/2026 3:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 253 Southwest St, Bellevue, OH 44811, USA Have you ever wanted to get more active with our museum but just didn’t know where to start? Do you have any friends that have expressed an interest in becoming an active member? On Saturday May 30th, we can help you do just that. An introductory seminar will be held in the Freight House at 1 pm to welcome new members and introduce participants to the tasks required to keep the museum functioning. Topics covered will include: Brief history of the museum Explanation of tasks and projects volunteers are needed for Brief overview of our rules and polices Walk around tour of the buildings and grounds weather permitting Q&A and suggestion period. We’ll try to keep the seminar to less than 3 hours in length, material covered may vary depending on the amount of attendees. We look forward to meeting you! https://madrivermuseum.org/events/ Not a member? Please join our team. https://madrivermuseum.org/membership/ Our insurance provider and bylaws requires all our volunteers to be current members of the Mad River & NKP Railroad Society.
item.dc:creator: Mad River & NKP Railroad

item.title: McCutchenville Recycling Drop Off collection weekend - 06/06/2026 12:00am EDT to 06/06/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description: Location : 8919 OH-53, McCutchenville, OH 44844, USA Join us for the monthly Recycling Drop-Off collection weekends in McCutchenville and Harpster! You can drop off your recyclables on the 1st weekend of every month, starting Friday at 4 PM and ending Monday at 8 AM. We accept aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, glass of any color, newspapers, office paper, cardboard, chipboard, and books. Please ensure that shredded paper is bagged, and rinse your food and drink containers clean while leaving the caps on. For more details, visit: [McCutchenville Collection](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2367) or [Harpster Collection](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2315).

item.title: Seneca County Human Resources Association | - 06/09/2026 11:45am EDT to 06/09/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 45 St Lawrence Dr, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Seneca County Human Resources Association on Tuesday, June 9, 2026, from 11:45 a.m. to 1:00 p.m. in the McMullen Conference Room at Mercy Health – Tiffin Hospital. Eastman & Smith Ltd. will return to provide a mid-year legal update, reviewing recent legislative and regulatory developments and offering practical strategies to help HR professionals navigate evolving workplace requirements. The Seneca County Human Resources Association (SCHRA) connects HR professionals, business leaders, and organizational managers across Seneca County to share best practices, discuss workforce trends, and grow professionally. Meetings focus on practical learning, peer discussion, and updates on programs and resources that support hiring, engagement, compliance, and organizational success. Membership in the Seneca County Human Resources Association (SCHRA) is open to anyone with HR responsibilities or an interest in workforce and personnel development. SCHRA operates on a one-time yearly registration, allowing members to sign up once for the entire year rather than registering for each individual meeting. Registered members receive meeting notices, speaker details, and calendar invitations for every upcoming session throughout the year. To register for SCHRA visit: https://www.developsenecacounty.org/schra-interest-form/
item.dc:creator: Seneca Regional Chamber & Development

item.description: Location : 33 N Marion St, Bloomville, OH 44818, USA Join us as we take a deep dive on small sections of the bible for our monthly bible study. Location and menu vary month to month. Call or text Audrey at 419-561-0244 if you'd like to join us! We have a very casual, fun, "no stupid questions" atmosphere. It's not hard to jump in - you don't need to have joined a previously. Come and go as you please!

item.title: Holiday Open Studio - 06/15/2026 9:00am EDT to 06/22/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 1034 Township Rd 293, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA

I'm a formerly trained Northwest Ohio artist with a specialty in landscape paintings. On MONdays and Thursdays until Christmas, I’ll have my studio and gallery space at my rural home in the Middle Of Nowhere (MON) open to artists, art lovers, and enthusiasts to chat, and see what I have on my easel.

December sales. December treats. Expect art, wine, refreshments and goodies. Join me for some lively convo on all things art!

This "open studio day" started out as an accountability exercise on my part (to make sure I would be in my studio achieving my goals), but has since become a fun interaction I cherish.

To see more about my artwork : www.MONgallery.us

*Please drive all the way up between the studio door and the brown barn… easy parking for you. There are stairs down into the studio that may be hindering for those with mobility issues, but there is a handrail.
item.dc:creator: MONgallery and Art Studio

item.title: 2026 Birthday (Gun) Raffle - 06/20/2026 7:00pm EDT to 06/20/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 120 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA RAFFLE Its official! We will be holding our first large item raffle. Both were locally manufactured or built: Grand Prize: AR15 (Anderson lower with a Magpul stock and grip; 16 inch Ballistic Advantage 1/7 5.56 barrel; Anderson enhanced lower parts kit) Built and painted by local Daniel Cole (Army) Consolation Prize: Customs Fire Pit (approximately 28x28in) fabricated by #PriceManufacturingLLC will be sold at $10/ea Holder swears/affirms they are legally allowed to own/possess forearm IAW State of Ohio and Federal Laws (for firearm, anyone can by a ticket to win pit). Winner must complete necessary paperwork. Winner will have to submit a background check to Johnson's Hunting & Fishing on State Rte 53 in McCutchenville. All fees paid by raffle proceeds. will be picked up at same location upon completion and legal requirements. tickets are on sale both physical and online. Drawing will be held June 20th, 2026. $10/ea Legal Disclaimer: Dress Right Dress Inc. does NOT sell or manufacture these items. The agency is the mere beneficiary of said raffle proceeds. Any damage, physical or mental harm is at the responsibility of the winners of each prize, thus waiving all legal responsibility from the organization, officers, Board of Directors, their heirs, or personal properties. Also waiving responsibility from those partners and manufacturers. Ticket holders must live in Ohio. Winners must be able to pick up items. Winner must submit and successfully complete a background check through: Johnson's Hunting & Fishing 8501 County Hwy 16 Mccutchenville, Ohio 44844 Contact Dress Right Dress for other ticket options.
item.dc:creator: Dress, Right, Dress

item.title: Out of Eden-A Tribute To The Eagles Summer Concert - 06/20/2026 7:00pm EDT to 06/20/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 167 E Market St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Get ready for a great night of classic rock in Downtown Tiffin! Join us for Out of Eden – A Tribute to the Eagles on Saturday, June 20 at 7:00 PM at the East Green Amphitheatre. Sing along to all your favorite Eagles hits in a fun outdoor concert atmosphere with friends, family, and the community. This is a free community concert sponsored by Simply Shop Local, celebrating summer and supporting our local businesses. Bring a chair or blanket, enjoy the evening, and experience the music that defined a generation right here in Tiffin. Rain Location: If the weather doesn’t cooperate, the concert will move to The Ritz Theatre in Downtown Tiffin. Mark your calendar, invite your friends, and come celebrate the sounds of the Eagles with us!
item.dc:creator: Simply Shop Local

item.description: Location : 928 W Market St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for the Seneca County Safety Council June meeting on June 23, 2026 at 12:00 PM at the North Central Ohio Educational Service Center. item.title: Chamber & Development Chat and Assist | June 2026 - 06/24/2026 10:00am EDT to 06/24/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA We've opened the doors to provide a better member experience for our chamber & development members and to help you get the most of your membership, ask questions, and get help to elevate your business! Chamber & Development Chat and Assist is an opportunity for our members to meet one on one with a Chamber & Development team member to understand and take advantage everything that comes with their membership, the opportunity to get help with anything from social media tips to Canva crash courses and beyond, to providing feedback to us so we can get better! Upcoming 2026 Chat & Assist dates include: Wednesday, July 22, 2026 from 10 AM to 1 PM Wednesday, August 26, 2026 from 10 AM to 1 PM Wednesday, September 23, 2026 from 10 AM to 1 PM Wednesday, October 28, 2026 from 10 AM to 1 PM You can secure a time (this is just so we know who to expect and how to best prepare!) at https://calendar.google.com/.../AcZssZ2Jz5jSs5OdbQ7h4VKho....

item.title: LIFEGUARDING - 06/26/2026 12:00am EDT to 06/28/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 180 Summit St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Ready to make a difference this summer? 🌊☀️

Become a certified lifeguard and gain the skills to save lives, build confidence, and become a leader!

Important Details:

JUNE 26-28, 2026

Friday 4p-8p, Saturday 8a-5p, & Sunday 8a-5p

• Blended course format – 7 hours of online coursework must be completed before the first day of class

• Swim assessment required one week prior to the first class- Friday at 6pm

• Attendance is mandatory for all scheduled days (no make-up sessions)

• Registration closes one week before the class begins

• Limited to just 8 students per class – reserve your spot early!

Don’t wait—spots fill fast! 📅 Enroll now at the Tiffin Community YMCA and dive into a new opportunity!
item.dc:creator: YMCA of Bucyrus-Tiffin, Inc

item.description: Location : 11385 Co Hwy 4, Carey, OH 43316, USA Join us for the Recycling Drop Off Collection Weekend, where you can dispose of your recyclables every month on the 4th weekend. The collection is available from Friday at 4 PM through Monday at 8 AM. We accept aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, glass of any color, newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard, and books. Please bag any shredded paper and ensure that food and drink containers are rinsed clean; caps may remain on items. For more information visit our event calendar.
item.dc:creator: Wyandot County Recycling Center

item.title: Downtown Tiffin Clean Up ~ June 2026 - 06/27/2026 9:00am EDT to 06/27/2026 10:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for a Downtown Tiffin Clean-Up on Saturday, June 27, 2026, from 9:00–10:30 AM! We’ll meet at Cherry Alley, and we’ll have everything you need (trash bags, brooms, dust-pans, and gloves). Who can volunteer? Everyone! Come on your own, bring a friend, or build a group and join "Bunky’s Litter League", a community-wide initiative focused on litter cleanup and beautification. Teams can include: Businesses Neighborhood groups Faith-based organizations Civic and service groups Clubs Families, individuals, and more... Volunteers are what make this event possible. Your help keeps downtown clean, welcoming, and vibrant. Please register to volunteer by clicking the “Ticket” tab. Save the dates (more clean-ups!) September 12, 2026 November 21, 2026 (Leaf Clean-Up)
item.dc:creator: Downtown Tiffin

item.description: Location : 25 Shaffer Park Dr, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Come meet with the management of Your Legacy Federal Credit Union every first Wednesday of the month at 11AM. This is a chance for members to have a cup of coffee and come voice their concerns, praises, or just ask questions. Eligible Members of the community are welcome to learn more about the "Credit Union Difference" and find out how our 70+ year Seneca County institution can help you with your current finances and future plans!

item.title: Financial Fitness Friday - 07/03/2026 8:30am EDT to 07/03/2026 5:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 25 Shaffer Park Dr, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join our Financial Counselor Rusti Claydon for Financial Fitness Friday, a new monthly event for our membership for FREE walk-in assistance and education surrounding your financial concerns! The first Friday of each month, Rusti has cleared her schedule to allow for members of the credit union to stop in and ask questions about their situation, ask for insights into how we can assist and how to better utilize Your Legacy Federal Credit Union offerings to their benefit! Not a member? Rusti can get you started with just a $5 minimum initial share. See you there!
item.dc:creator: Your Legacy Federal Credit Union

item.title: McCutchenville Recycling Drop Off collection weekend - 07/04/2026 12:00am EDT to 07/04/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description: Location : 8919 OH-53, McCutchenville, OH 44844, USA Join us for the McCutchenville Recycling Drop Off collection, held on the first weekend of every month from Friday at 4 PM until Monday at 8 AM. You can responsibly dispose of your recyclables, including aluminum and steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, any color of glass, newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard, and books. Please ensure that shredded paper is bagged and that food and drink containers are rinsed clean; caps can remain on the items. Note that the Wyandot County Recycling Center will be closed on Independence Day. For more details, visit [Wyandot County Calendar](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2368).
item.dc:creator: Wyandot County Recycling Center

item.title: Holiday Open Studio - 07/13/2026 9:00am EDT to 07/20/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 1034 Township Rd 293, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA

I'm a formerly trained Northwest Ohio artist with a specialty in landscape paintings. On MONdays and Thursdays until Christmas, I’ll have my studio and gallery space at my rural home in the Middle Of Nowhere (MON) open to artists, art lovers, and enthusiasts to chat, and see what I have on my easel.

December sales. December treats. Expect art, wine, refreshments and goodies. Join me for some lively convo on all things art!

This "open studio day" started out as an accountability exercise on my part (to make sure I would be in my studio achieving my goals), but has since become a fun interaction I cherish.

To see more about my artwork : www.MONgallery.us

*Please drive all the way up between the studio door and the brown barn… easy parking for you. There are stairs down into the studio that may be hindering for those with mobility issues, but there is a handrail.
item.dc:creator: MONgallery and Art Studio

item.title: LIFEGUARDING COURSE - 07/17/2026 12:00am EDT to 07/19/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 180 Summit St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Ready to make a difference this summer? 🌊☀️

Become a certified lifeguard and gain the skills to save lives, build confidence, and become a leader!

Important Details:

JULY 17-19, 2026

Friday 4p-8p, Saturday 8a-5p, & Sunday 8a-5p

• Blended course format – 7 hours of online coursework must be completed before the first day of class

• Swim assessment required one week prior to the first class- Friday at 6pm

• Attendance is mandatory for all scheduled days (no make-up sessions)

• Registration closes one week before the class begins

• Limited to just 8 students per class – reserve your spot early!

Don’t wait—spots fill fast! 📅 Enroll now at the Tiffin Community YMCA and dive into a new opportunity!
item.dc:creator: YMCA of Bucyrus-Tiffin, Inc

item.title: Chamber & Development Chat and Assist | July 2026 - 07/22/2026 10:00am EDT to 07/22/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA We've opened the doors to provide a better member experience for our chamber & development members and to help you get the most of your membership, ask questions, and get help to elevate your business! Chamber & Development Chat and Assist is an opportunity for our members to meet one on one with a Chamber & Development team member to understand and take advantage everything that comes with their membership, the opportunity to get help with anything from social media tips to Canva crash courses and beyond, to providing feedback to us so we can get better! Upcoming 2026 Chat & Assist dates include: Wednesday, August 26, 2026 from 10 AM to 1 PM Wednesday, September 23, 2026 from 10 AM to 1 PM Wednesday, October 28, 2026 from 10 AM to 1 PM You can secure a time (this is just so we know who to expect and how to best prepare!) at https://calendar.google.com/.../AcZssZ2Jz5jSs5OdbQ7h4VKho....

item.title: McCutchenville Recycling Drop Off collection weekend - 08/01/2026 12:00am EDT to 08/01/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description: Location : 8919 OH-53, McCutchenville, OH 44844, USA Join us for the monthly Recycling Drop-Off Collection Weekend, held on the first weekend of each month from Friday at 4 PM to Monday at 8 AM. You can drop off your recyclables at both McCutchenville and Harpster locations. Accepted items include aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, various colors of glass, newspapers, office paper, cardboard, chipboard, and books. Please ensure that shredded paper is bagged and that food and drink containers are rinsed clean, although caps can be left on. For more details, visit the respective event links: [McCutchenville](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2369) and [Harpster](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2317).

item.title: 2026 Walter A. Daniel, MD Memorial Golf Outing - 08/03/2026 8:00am EDT to 08/03/2026 3:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 4399 OH-231, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Mercy Health Foundation is hosting it's 35th Annual Walter A. Daniel, MD Memorial Golf Outing, in support of Mercy Health - Tiffin & Willard Hospitals, will take place on Monday, August 3rd at Mohawk Golf & Country Club. It is a full day of fun and golf!

8:00 a.m - Registration/Driving Range Open

8:45 a.m. - Opening Announcements

9:00 a.m. - Tee Time

For sponsorship and recognition opportunities, please visit tinyurl.com/TiffinWillardGolf2026 to secure your spot!

For any questions about the event, please contact Jace Abel, Special Events Coordinator, at jabel@mercy.com or (567) 938-9429.

Dr. Walter A. Daniel was instrumental in establishing the Mercy Health Foundation — Tiffin in December of 1985. His outstanding dedication to rural health care will always be remembered. His example of philanthropy through leadership and generosity continues to thrive through the Mercy Health Foundation in the Tiffin and Willard communities and beyond. We honor his memory through the naming of this annual tournament that provides a time for fellowship as well as philanthropy.

Note of appreciation - The Leadership and associates of the Mercy Health — Tiffin and Willard Hospitals express sincere gratitude for your participation in the Walter A. Daniel, MD Memorial Golf Outing. We treasure your continuous support of Mercy Health and are committed to providing excellent health care to the Tiffin and Willard communities.
item.dc:creator: Mercy Health Foundation

This is a chance for members to have a cup of coffee and come voice their concerns, praises, or just ask questions. item.title: 2026 Educators Appreciation Breakfast - 08/06/2026 7:00am EDT to 08/06/2026 9:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 2460 OH-231, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for the 2026 Educators Appreciation Breakfast! This event allows the Seneca County community the opportunity to welcome new educators from all of the surrounding schools and universities. The Outstanding Educator of the Year award will also be presented. The doors will open at 7 AM with breakfast buffet lines opening at 7:15 AM. We ask that attendees grab their name tags at the entrance and get breakfast on their way to their seats. This event is open seating.

item.title: Financial Fitness Friday - 08/07/2026 8:30am EDT to 08/07/2026 5:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 25 Shaffer Park Dr, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join our Financial Counselor Rusti Claydon for Financial Fitness Friday, a new monthly event for our membership for FREE walk-in assistance and education surrounding your financial concerns! The first Friday of each month, Rusti has cleared her schedule to allow for members of the credit union to stop in and ask questions about their situation, ask for insights into how we can assist and how to better utilize Your Legacy Federal Credit Union offerings to their benefit! Not a member? Rusti can get you started with just a $5 minimum initial share. See you there!
