item.title: Holiday Open Studio - 08/10/2026 9:00am EDT to 08/17/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 1034 Township Rd 293, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA

I'm a formerly trained Northwest Ohio artist with a specialty in landscape paintings. On MONdays and Thursdays until Christmas, I’ll have my studio and gallery space at my rural home in the Middle Of Nowhere (MON) open to artists, art lovers, and enthusiasts to chat, and see what I have on my easel.

December sales. December treats. Expect art, wine, refreshments and goodies. Join me for some lively convo on all things art!

This "open studio day" started out as an accountability exercise on my part (to make sure I would be in my studio achieving my goals), but has since become a fun interaction I cherish.

To see more about my artwork : www.MONgallery.us

*Please drive all the way up between the studio door and the brown barn… easy parking for you. There are stairs down into the studio that may be hindering for those with mobility issues, but there is a handrail.
item.dc:creator: MONgallery and Art Studio

item.title: Downtown Tiffin Town Hall Meetings: August 2026 - 08/10/2026 7:00pm EDT to 08/10/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for the next session of the Downtown Tiffin Town Hall Series: Connecting People, Ideas, and Progress on Monday, August 10, 2026, hosted by the Business Enhancement Committee at 7 PM. This session, titled “Empower Downtown: Tools, Resources & Collaboration for Success,” will focus on business development and support. Discover local, state, and national programs designed to strengthen downtown businesses—from workforce initiatives and job postings to grants, mentorship, training, and networking opportunities that foster collaboration and long-term success.
item.dc:creator: Downtown Tiffin

item.title: Nonprofit Leadership Alliance | Advocacy in Action: Engaging Local Leaders - 08/14/2026 11:00am EDT to 08/14/2026 12:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : Gillmor Student Center, 190 Miami St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Seneca Regional Chamber & Development and United Way of North Central Ohio for the third Nonprofit Leadership Alliance quarterly meeting of 2026 on Friday, August 14 at 11 AM, hosted at Osceola Theatre at Tiffin University. This session is designed for nonprofit leaders, staff, and board members who want to better understand local decision-making and strengthen their ability to advocate for their organizations and the communities they serve. The featured topic, “Advocacy in Action: Engaging Local Leaders,” will bring together local and state leaders for a moderated discussion focused on public priorities, community challenges, and opportunities for collaboration with the nonprofit sector. Attendees will gain insight into how decisions are made and how nonprofits can effectively engage with elected officials and public leaders. The second half of the session will offer an interactive opportunity for nonprofit attendees to connect directly with the panelists—asking questions, sharing challenges, and practicing advocacy in a supportive, relationship-focused setting. These meetings are free and open to all nonprofit employees and board members in Seneca County. Chamber & Development membership is not required. Take advantage of this opportunity to build relationships, strengthen your advocacy skills, and engage directly with local leaders. Register now to secure your spot!
item.dc:creator: United Way of North Central Ohio

item.title: Wharton Recycling Drop Off Collection Weekend - 08/22/2026 12:00am EDT to 08/22/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description: Location : 11385 Co Hwy 4, Carey, OH 43316, USA Join us for the Recycling Drop Off Collection Weekend, a monthly event held on the 4th weekend from Friday at 4 PM to Monday at 8 AM at various locations. This event accepts a wide range of recyclables including aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, any color of glass, newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard, and books. Shredded paper should be bagged, and food and drink containers must be rinsed clean. You may leave the caps on your items. For more details, visit our event calendar links: [Wharton](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2526) and [Kirby](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2474).

item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA We've opened the doors to provide a better member experience for our chamber & development members and to help you get the most of your membership, ask questions, and get help to elevate your business! Chamber & Development Chat and Assist is an opportunity for our members to meet one on one with a Chamber & Development team member to understand and take advantage everything that comes with their membership, the opportunity to get help with anything from social media tips to Canva crash courses and beyond, to providing feedback to us so we can get better! Upcoming 2026 Chat & Assist dates include: Wednesday, September 23, 2026 from 10 AM to 1 PM Wednesday, October 28, 2026 from 10 AM to 1 PM You can secure a time (this is just so we know who to expect and how to best prepare!) at https://calendar.google.com/.../AcZssZ2Jz5jSs5OdbQ7h4VKho....

item.title: McCutchenville Recycling Drop Off collection weekend - 09/05/2026 12:00am EDT to 09/05/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description: Location : 8919 OH-53, McCutchenville, OH 44844, USA Join us for the monthly Recycling Drop-Off collection weekends in McCutchenville and Harpster! Every first weekend of the month, from Friday at 4 PM to Monday at 8 AM, residents can drop off their recyclables. Accepted items include aluminum and steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, all colors of glass, newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard, and books. Please ensure that shredded paper is bagged and that food and drink containers are rinsed clean (caps can remain on). For further details, visit the following links: [McCutchenville](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2370) and [Harpster](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2318).

item.title: Downtown Tiffin Clean Up ~ September 2026 - 09/12/2026 9:00am EDT to 09/12/2026 10:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for a Downtown Tiffin Clean-Up on Saturday, September 12, 2026, from 9:00–10:30 AM! We’ll meet at Cherry Alley, and we’ll have everything you need (trash bags, brooms, dust-pans, and gloves). Who can volunteer? Everyone! Come on your own, bring a friend, or build a group and join "Bunky’s Litter League", a community-wide initiative focused on litter cleanup and beautification. Teams can include: Businesses Neighborhood groups Faith-based organizations Civic and service groups Clubs Families, individuals, and more... Volunteers are what make this event possible. Your help keeps downtown clean, welcoming, and vibrant. Please register to volunteer by clicking the “Ticket” tab. Save the date (last clean-up!) November 21, 2026 (Leaf Clean-Up)
item.dc:creator: Downtown Tiffin

item.title: Holiday Open Studio - 09/14/2026 9:00am EDT to 09/21/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 1034 Township Rd 293, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA

I'm a formerly trained Northwest Ohio artist with a specialty in landscape paintings. On MONdays and Thursdays until Christmas, I’ll have my studio and gallery space at my rural home in the Middle Of Nowhere (MON) open to artists, art lovers, and enthusiasts to chat, and see what I have on my easel.

December sales. December treats. Expect art, wine, refreshments and goodies. Join me for some lively convo on all things art!

This "open studio day" started out as an accountability exercise on my part (to make sure I would be in my studio achieving my goals), but has since become a fun interaction I cherish.

To see more about my artwork : www.MONgallery.us

*Please drive all the way up between the studio door and the brown barn… easy parking for you. There are stairs down into the studio that may be hindering for those with mobility issues, but there is a handrail.
item.dc:creator: MONgallery and Art Studio

item.title: Chamber & Development Chat and Assist | September 2026 - 09/23/2026 10:00am EDT to 09/23/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA We've opened the doors to provide a better member experience for our chamber & development members and to help you get the most of your membership, ask questions, and get help to elevate your business! Chamber & Development Chat and Assist is an opportunity for our members to meet one on one with a Chamber & Development team member to understand and take advantage everything that comes with their membership, the opportunity to get help with anything from social media tips to Canva crash courses and beyond, to providing feedback to us so we can get better! Upcoming 2026 Chat & Assist dates include: Wednesday, October 28, 2026 from 10 AM to 1 PM You can secure a time (this is just so we know who to expect and how to best prepare!) at https://calendar.google.com/.../AcZssZ2Jz5jSs5OdbQ7h4VKho....
item.dc:creator: Seneca Regional Chamber & Development

item.title: Wharton Recycling Drop Off Collection Weekend - 09/26/2026 12:00am EDT to 09/26/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description: Location : 11385 Co Hwy 4, Carey, OH 43316, USA Join us for the Recycling Drop Off Collection Weekend, held on the 4th weekend of every month at two locations: Wharton and Kirby. You can drop off your recyclables from 4 PM on Friday until 8 AM on Monday. Accepted items include aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, any color of glass, newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard, and books. Please ensure that shredded paper is bagged and that food and drink containers are rinsed clean (caps can remain on). For more details, visit [Wharton](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2527) and [Kirby](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2475).
item.dc:creator: Wyandot County Recycling Center

item.title: McCutchenville Recycling Drop Off collection weekend - 10/03/2026 12:00am EDT to 10/03/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description: Location : 8919 OH-53, McCutchenville, OH 44844, USA Join us for the Recycling Drop Off collection weekend, occurring on the first weekend of every month from Friday at 4 PM to Monday at 8 AM, at both McCutchenville and Harpster locations. We accept a variety of recyclables including aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, all colors of glass, newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard, and books. Please ensure that shredded paper is bagged, and food and drink containers are rinsed clean. Caps can remain on items. For more details, visit [McCutchenville](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2371) or [Harpster](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2319).

item.title: BUCC's Beer, Burgers & Bibles (Monthly Bible Study) - 10/14/2026 6:00pm EDT to 10/14/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 33 N Marion St, Bloomville, OH 44818, USA Join us as we take a deep dive on small sections of the bible for our monthly bible study. Location and menu vary month to month. Call or text Audrey at 419-561-0244 if you'd like to join us! We have a very casual, fun, "no stupid questions" atmosphere. It's not hard to jump in - you don't need to have joined a previously. Come and go as you please!

item.title: Wharton Recycling Drop Off Collection Weekend - 10/24/2026 12:00am EDT to 10/24/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description: Location : 11385 Co Hwy 4, Carey, OH 43316, USA Join us for the Recycling Drop Off Collection Weekend, taking place every even month on the 4th weekend, from 4 PM on Friday through 8 AM on Monday. This event allows you to responsibly dispose of recyclables such as aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, any color of glass, newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard, and books. Please bag shredded paper, rinse food and drink containers, and feel free to leave the caps on your items. For more information, visit [Wyandot County Calendar](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2528) and [Kirby Recycling Collection](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2476).

item.title: Chamber & Development Chat and Assist | October 2026 - 10/28/2026 10:00am EDT to 10/28/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA We've opened the doors to provide a better member experience for our chamber & development members and to help you get the most of your membership, ask questions, and get help to elevate your business! Chamber & Development Chat and Assist is an opportunity for our members to meet one on one with a Chamber & Development team member to understand and take advantage everything that comes with their membership, the opportunity to get help with anything from social media tips to Canva crash courses and beyond, to providing feedback to us so we can get better! You can secure a time (this is just so we know who to expect and how to best prepare!) at https://calendar.google.com/.../AcZssZ2Jz5jSs5OdbQ7h4VKho....

item.description: Location : 8919 OH-53, McCutchenville, OH 44844, USA Join us for the monthly Recycling Drop-Off Collection Weekend, held on the first weekend of every month from Friday at 4 PM to Monday at 8 AM. You can drop off your recyclables at both McCutchenville and Harpster locations. Accepted items include aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, any color of glass, newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard, and books. Please ensure that shredded paper is bagged and that food and drink containers are rinsed clean. Caps can remain on your recyclable items. For more details, visit the official Wyandot County calendar: [Event Details](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2372).

item.title: Downtown Tiffin Town Hall Meetings: November 2026 - 11/10/2026 7:00pm EST to 11/10/2026 8:00pm EST
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for the final session of the Downtown Tiffin Town Hall Series: Connecting People, Ideas, and Progress on Tuesday, November 10, 2026, hosted by the Downtown Development Board (DDB)at 7 PM. This session, titled “Downtown Reflections: Celebrating Wins & Shaping What’s Next,” will focus on review and community dialogue. Join the conversation as we look back on the year’s progress, discuss challenges and opportunities, and share ideas to shape the future of Downtown Tiffin. This open forum will provide space for feedback, inspiration, and collaboration as we continue building a vibrant and connected downtown community.
item.dc:creator: Downtown Tiffin

item.title: Major General Lorenzo Dow Gasser, Seneca County’s Forgotten General - 11/15/2026 2:00pm EST to 11/15/2026 3:30pm EST
item.description: Location : 28 Clay St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Lorenzo Gasser was born in Lykens, Ohio to an immigrant farmer and mother who died shortly after giving birth. Orphaned at a young age he was raised in Tiffin, Ohio. Despite these early setbacks General Gasser found a life in the Army, serving in three wars and numerous expeditions while rising to the rank of Major General and Deputy Chief of Staff of the Army. Come hear the remarkable story of a local boy who lived the American Dream.
item.dc:creator: Seneca County Museum

item.title: Downtown Tiffin (Leaf) Clean Up ~ November 2026 - 11/21/2026 9:00am EST to 11/21/2026 10:30am EST
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for a Downtown Tiffin "Leaf" Clean-Up on Saturday, November 21, 2026, from 9:00–10:30 AM! We’ll meet at Cherry Alley, and we’ll have everything you need (rakes, trash bags, brooms, dust-pans, and gloves). Bring a leaf blower if you have one. Who can volunteer? Everyone! Come on your own, bring a friend, or build a group and join "Bunky’s Litter League", a community-wide initiative focused on litter cleanup and beautification. Teams can include: Businesses Neighborhood groups Faith-based organizations Civic and service groups Clubs Families, individuals, and more... Volunteers are what make this event possible. Your help keeps downtown clean, welcoming, and vibrant. Please register to volunteer by clicking the “Ticket” tab. Help make Downtown Tiffin sparkle for the holidays!
item.dc:creator: Downtown Tiffin

item.title: Seneca County Human Resources Association | December Meeting - 12/08/2026 11:45am EST to 12/08/2026 1:00pm EST
item.description: Location : 45 St Lawrence Dr, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Seneca County Human Resources Association for our annual December Holiday Gathering on Tuesday, December 8, 2026, from 11:45 a.m. to 1:00 p.m. in the McMullen Conference Room at Mercy Health – Tiffin Hospital. This gathering is a great opportunity to connect with fellow HR professionals, celebrate the end of the year, and enjoy time together as a group. The Seneca County Human Resources Association (SCHRA) connects HR professionals, business leaders, and organizational managers across Seneca County to share best practices, discuss workforce trends, and grow professionally. Meetings focus on practical learning, peer discussion, and updates on programs and resources that support hiring, engagement, compliance, and organizational success. Membership in the Seneca County Human Resources Association (SCHRA) is open to anyone with HR responsibilities or an interest in workforce and personnel development. SCHRA operates on a one-time yearly registration, allowing members to sign up once for the entire year rather than registering for each individual meeting. Registered members receive meeting notices, speaker details, and calendar invitations for every upcoming session throughout the year. To register for SCHRA visit: https://www.developsenecacounty.org/schra-interest-form/
item.dc:creator: Seneca Regional Chamber & Development

item.description: Location : 11385 Co Hwy 4, Carey, OH 43316, USA **Recycling Drop-Off Collection Weekend** Join us for Recycling Drop-Off Collection Weekend, occurring every month on the 4th weekend from Friday at 4 PM to Monday at 8 AM. Participants can drop off a variety of recyclables including aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, glass (in any color), newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard, and books. We request that shredded paper be bagged and that all food and drink containers are rinsed clean, although caps can remain on the items. For specific event dates and details, please visit the respective calendar links for Wharton and Kirby communities.
item.dc:creator: Wyandot County Recycling Center

item.title: McCutchenville Recycling Drop Off collection weekend - 01/02/2027 12:00am EST to 01/02/2027 11:59pm EST
item.description: Location : 8919 OH-53, McCutchenville, OH 44844, USA Participate in the monthly Recycling Drop-Off collection weekends in McCutchenville and Harpster! You can conveniently drop off your recyclables every first weekend of the month, starting Friday at 4 PM until Monday at 8 AM. Accepted materials include aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, all colors of glass, newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard, and books. Please bag shredded paper and ensure that food and drink containers are rinsed clean. Caps can remain on your items. For more details, visit the Wyandot County calendar: [McCutchenville](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2374) | [Harpster](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2322).

item.description: Location : 11385 Co Hwy 4, Carey, OH 43316, USA **Recycling Drop-Off Collection Weekend** Join us for a community recycling drop-off event every month on the 4th weekend, specifically from Friday at 4 PM to Monday at 8 AM. We also ask that your food and drink containers are rinsed clean. You can leave the caps on your items. https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2539
item.dc:creator: Wyandot County Recycling Center
